outlook.headline: AI-driven NAND recovery broadens despite softer mention momentum
outlook.summary: Company reads skew clearly positive: AI data-center demand, higher-layer QLC adoption, and tight supply are pulling NAND into an early recovery phase. The improvement looks broadest for Micron, SanDisk, Lam, MKS, and the equipment vendors seeing upgrades, while neutral names suggest the cycle is not yet uniformly strong. The main caveat is that mention intensity has rolled over from 2.2x to 1.6x, so the next 1-2 quarters depend on whether current demand converts into sustained orders rather than a short-lived burst.

drivers.label: AI data-center NAND demand accelerating
drivers.detail: AI is increasing NAND's role in the memory hierarchy and lifting data-center demand for high-capacity parts.

drivers.label: QLC mix shift and higher-layer content
drivers.detail: Rising demand for higher-layer QLC is improving the product mix and supporting supplier exposure.

drivers.label: Supply remains tight
drivers.detail: Tight supply is keeping the market supported and reinforcing pricing/leverage for vendors and OEMs.

drivers.label: Early signs of recovery in equipment demand
drivers.detail: NAND upgrades and related revenue are starting to pick up, suggesting the cycle is turning up from a low base.

drivers.label: Mention momentum is easing
drivers.detail: The trend has fallen to 1.6x from 2.2x, indicating the narrative is losing some urgency even as fundamentals improve.

risks: AI-related demand may not sustain if customer spending pauses after initial build-out.
If wafer starts or upgrades rise too quickly, tight supply could normalize and slow the recovery.
The weaker mention trend could foreshadow a near-term lull in incremental positive updates.
